chapter_title.ar: بَابُ الصَّائِمِ يَتَقَيَّأُ أَوْ يَذْرَعُهُ الْقَيْ‏ءُ أَوْ يَقْلِسُ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 27 – The Fasting one vomits or nauseates the vomit, or regurgitates

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: صحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (6/287)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: صحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (6/287)
text: سُئِلَ أَبُو جَعْفَرٍ ( عليه السلام ) عَنِ الْقَلْسِ يُفَطِّرُ الصَّائِمَ قَالَ لَا .

en.hubeali: Muhammad Bin Yahya, from Muhammad Bin Al Husayn, from Ali Bin Al Hakam, from Al A’ala Bin Razeyn, from Muhammad Bin Muslim who said,
‘Abu Ja’far<sup>asws</sup> was asked about the regurgitation, would the Fasting one break his Fast?’ He<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘No’.
en.sarwar: 5. Muhammad ibn Yahya has narrated from Muhammad ibn al-Husayn, from Ali ibn al-Hakam from al-‘Ala’ ibn Razin from Muhammad ibn Muslim who has said the following: “Abu Ja’far, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, was asked about burping if it invalidates the fast. He (the Imam) said, ‘No, it does not invalidate one’s fast.’”
